summary: Completed testing of RouterOS v6/v7 API Mode feature. All backend APIs (10/10 tests) and frontend functionality (100%) working correctly. Device dialog correctly shows API Mode selector with auto port/SSL switching. Device cards display correct API Mode badges.

minor.issue: Legacy devices without api_mode field return 'NOT SET' but backend defaults to 'rest' mode correctly
minor.fix_applied: Backend defaults work correctly - not a bug, just legacy data compatibility

passed_tests: ✅ Backend: Health endpoint returns OK
✅ Backend: Admin login with admin/admin123 works
✅ Backend: List devices returns api_mode field
✅ Backend: Create device with api_mode='rest' (port 443, SSL true)
✅ Backend: Create device with api_mode='api' (port 8728, SSL false)
✅ Backend: Update device api_mode from REST to API protocol
✅ Backend: Default api_mode is 'rest' when not specified
✅ Backend: test-api endpoint works and returns mode info
✅ Backend: test-new endpoint works for both API modes
✅ Backend: Existing devices have valid api_mode field
✅ Frontend: Login and navigation to Devices page works
✅ Frontend: Add Device dialog displays API Mode selector
✅ Frontend: Default values: REST API mode, port 443, HTTPS
✅ Frontend: Switching to API Protocol mode auto-changes port to 8728 and SSL to false
✅ Frontend: Switching back to REST API auto-changes port to 443 and SSL to true
✅ Frontend: Helper text updates based on selected mode
✅ Frontend: Device creation saves api_mode field correctly to database
✅ Frontend: Device card shows 'RouterOS 7+ (REST)' badge for REST mode
✅ Frontend: Device card shows 'RouterOS 6+ (API)' badge for API protocol mode
✅ Frontend: Edit device dialog preserves and displays correct api_mode
seed_data_creation: No seed data created. Tested with existing 'Router Core' device.
context_for_next_testing_agent: API mode feature fully implemented and tested. DevicesPage.jsx has API Mode selector with auto port/SSL switching. Backend mikrotik_api.py has get_api_client factory function that returns MikroTikRestAPI (v7) or MikroTikRouterAPI (v6) based on api_mode. Device cards correctly display API mode badges.

features_verified.Device dialog form displays API Mode sel: PASS - Shows 'REST API (RouterOS 7.1+)' and 'API Protocol (RouterOS 6.x+)' options
features_verified.Device creation saves api_mode field cor: PASS - Verified via GET after POST
features_verified.Backend test-api endpoint works for both: PASS - Returns mode info in response
features_verified.Edit device preserves and displays corre: PASS - Verified with existing Router Core device
features_verified.API mode helper text updates based on se: PASS - Shows correct Indonesian text for each mode
